ref: May he bless you with the land he promised Abraham, so that you will take over this land where we now live as foreigners.
text: na ɔmfa Abraham nhyira nhyira wo, wo ne w’asefoɔ a wɔka wo ho, na woanya asase a woyɛ so hɔhoɔ a Onyankopɔn de ama Abraham yi.
translated: and you will be blessed through Abraham, you are his descendants, and you will inherit the land he now lives in, which God gave to Abraham

ref: God said, “I command light to shine!” And light started shining.
text: Na Onyankopɔn kaa sɛ: Ɛnyɛ hann! Na ɛyɛɛ hann.
translated: And God said: Let there be light! And there was light.

ref: Meanwhile, Jacob heard what had happened. But his sons were out in the fields with the cattle, so he did not do anything at the time.
text: Na Yakob tee sɛ wagu ne babaa Dina ho fi. Nanso ɛnam sɛ na ne mmammarima no wɔ ne mmoa nkyɛn wɔ wiram no, enti ɔyɛɛ komm de kɔsii sɛ wɔbaa fie.
translated: And Jacob told his sons to bring her back home. But because his sons were grazing their flocks, he waited until they came home.

ref: But God came to Abimelech in a dream and said, “You have taken a married woman into your home, and for this you will die!”
text: Na Onyankopɔn baa Abimelek nkyɛn anadwo daeɛ mu, na ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ: Hwɛ, ɔbaa a woakɔfa no no nti, woawu awie, ɛfiri sɛ ɔyɛ ɔbarima bi yere.
translated: But God came to Abimelech in a dream one night and said to him, "You are as good as dead because of the woman you have taken; she is a married woman."

ref: I will remember my promise to you and to all other living creatures. Never again will I let floodwaters destroy all life.
text: na makae m’apam a ɛwɔ me ne mo, ne abɔdeɛ nyinaa mu ateasefoɔ ntam no, na nsuo annane nsuyire ansɛe abɔdeɛ nyinaa bio.
translated: and let your peace, which surpasses all understanding, guard my heart and your mind in Christ Jesus, and the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with all men

ref: A young Hebrew, who was a servant of the captain of the guard, was there with us at the time. When we told him our dreams, he explained what each of them meant,
text: Na Hebrini aberanteɛ bi a ɔyɛ ankɔbeafoɔ panin no akoa ne yɛn na ɛwɔ hɔ, na yɛkekaa yɛn daeɛ kyerɛɛ no no, ɔkyerɛɛ yɛn yɛn daeɛ no ase, sɛdeɛ ebiara teɛ.
translated: And a certain woman, who was a prophetess, had two daughters who were with us, and we told her our situation, and she told us the meaning of our situation, just as everything is.

ref: and the muscle on his hip joint had been injured. That's why even today the people of Israel don't eat the hip muscle of any animal.
text: Enti Israelfoɔ nni ntini a ɛwɔ asene mu akwaa tokuro mu no de bɛduru nnɛ, ɛfiri sɛ ɔkaa Yakob dwonkuo tokuro mu, n’asene mu ntini no so.
translated: And the children of Israel dwelt in Egypt in the land of Goshen, and they had possessions therein, and grew, and multiplied exceedingly.

ref: When Jacob crossed the Euphrates River and headed for the hill country of Gilead, he took with him everything he owned. Laban Catches Up with Jacob
text: Na ɔne deɛ ɔwɔ nyinaa dwanee, na ɔsɔre twaa asubɔnten no, na ɔde n’ani kyerɛɛ Gilead bepɔ. Laban ti Yakob
translated: And he took his kinsmen with him, and pursued him seven days' journey; and they overtook him in the mount Gilead. And Laban said to Jacob

ref: They said to one another, “Look, here comes the hero of those dreams!
text: Na wɔsesee wɔn ho wɔn ho sɛ: Hwɛ, ɔdaeɛsofoɔ no na ɔreba no.
translated: And they were told to be careful, saying: Look, the teacher and the student.

ref: But God said, “Abraham, don't worry about your slave woman and the boy. Just do what Sarah tells you. Isaac will inherit your family name,
text: Na Onyankopɔn ka kyerɛɛ Abraham sɛ: Mma abɔfra no ne w’afenaa asɛm no nnyɛ wo ya, na deɛ Sara ka kyerɛɛ woɔ nyinaa, tie, na Isak mu na wɔbɛfrɛ w’asefoɔ.
translated: And God said to Abraham: Let it be concerning the boy and your slave, whatever Sarah has said to you, listen, and through Isaac your descendants will be named.

ref: So God made the giant sea monsters and all the living creatures that swim in the ocean. He also made every kind of bird. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Na Onyankopɔn bɔɔ nsuboa akɛseɛ ne nketewa ahodoɔ sɛdeɛ wɔn su teɛ; ne ntakraboa nyinaa sɛdeɛ wɔn su teɛ. Na Onyankopɔn hunuu sɛ ɛyɛ.
translated: And God saw the great sea monsters and every living creature that moved, which the waters brought forth according to their kind; and God saw that it was good.

ref: He then kissed Rachel and started crying because he was so happy.
text: Na Yakob fee Rahel ano, na ɔmaa ne nne so sui.
translated: And Jacob loved Rachel, and he said, I will serve thee seven years for her hand.

ref: Jacob said to Laban, “The time is up, and I want to marry Rachel now!”
text: Na Yakob ka kyerɛɛ Laban sɛ: Fa me yere ma me, na me nna no adu, na mene no nna.
translated: And Jacob said to Laban, give me my wife that I may go in to her, for my time is completed

ref: When he told his father about this dream, his father became angry and said, “What's that supposed to mean? Are your mother and I and your brothers all going to come and bow down to you?”
text: Na ɔka kyerɛɛ n’agya ne ne nuanom no, n’agya kaa n’anim sɛ: Daeɛ bɛn na woaso yi? Kyerɛ sɛ, me ne wo na ne wo nuanom bɛba abɛkoto fam wɔ w’anim?
translated: And he told his father and his brothers, and his father rebuked him and said: What is this dream that you have dreamed? Shall we indeed come, your mother and your brothers, to bow down to the ground before you?

ref: Joseph answered, “I'm looking for my brothers who are watching the sheep. Can you tell me where they are?”
text: Na ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ: Me nuanom na merehwehwɛ wɔn; mesrɛ wo, kyerɛ me faako a wɔrehwɛ wɔn mmoa no.
translated: And he told them: My mother's sisters and I are looking for them; I am asking you, show me where they are grazing their sheep.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Na Onyankopɔn bɔɔ onipa wɔ ne suban so. Aane, Nyankopɔn suban so na ɔbɔɔ no, ɔbarima ne ɔbaa na ɔbɔɔ wɔn.
translated: And God created man in his own image. Male and female created he them, and God blessed them.

ref: Store up enough food both for yourself and for them.
text: Na wo deɛ, boaboa aduane nyinaa bi ano kɔ mu, na ayɛ wo ne wɔn aduane.
translated: It is you who, when all the food is finished, go in and eat yours and theirs.

ref: Abraham, from now on your wife's name will be Sarah instead of Sarai.
text: Na Onyankopɔn ka kyerɛɛ Abraham sɛ: Wo yere Sarai deɛ, mfrɛ no Sarai bio, na mmom Sara.
translated: And God said to Abraham, As for Sarai your wife, you shall not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall be her name.

ref: You know how hard I've worked for you, so let me take my wives and children and leave.”
text: Fa me yerenom ne me mma a wɔn nti mesomm woɔ no ma me. Wo ara wonim me som a masom woɔ.
translated: They know my name and my family, that's why they call me by my name. You also know me, that's why you call me.

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Na Yakob too ɛhɔ din Peni-el, ɛfiri sɛ ɔse: Me ne Onyankopɔn adi nhunuanimu, nso me kra anya nkwa.
translated: And Jacob called the name of the place Peniel, for he said: I have seen God face to face, and my life is preserved.

ref: “Don't hurt the boy or harm him in any way!” the angel said. “Now I know that you truly obey God, because you were willing to offer him your only son.”
text: Na ɔse: Mfa wo nsa nka abarimaa no, nso nyɛ no hwee. Afei na mahunu sɛ wosuro Onyankopɔn, na woankame me wo ba korɔ no.
translated: It is said: Take your hand out of the dwarf's mouth, and it will bite. Then you will know that you are serving God, and He will reward you in heaven.

ref: Rachel said, “I've struggled hard with my sister, and I've won!” So she named the boy Naphtali.
text: Na Rahel kaa sɛ: Ako akɛseɛ na me ne me nuabaa adie, nso matumi no. Na ɔtoo no din Naftali.
translated: And Rachel said: Give me a son, or else I will die. And she became pregnant and gave birth to a son, and she named him Naphtali.

ref: Abram returned after he had defeated King Chedorlaomer and the other kings. Then the king of Sodom went to meet Abram in Shaveh Valley, which is also known as King's Valley.
text: Na ɔsane firii Kedorlaomer ne ahemfo a ɛkeka ne ho so nkonimdie reba no, Sodom hene firi kɔhyiaa no wɔ Sawe bonhwa mu.
translated: And it came to pass when he returned from the defeat of Chedorlaomer and the kings who were with him, that the king of Sodom went out to meet him at the valley of Shaveh.

ref: and said, “Gentlemen, I am your servant. Please come to my home. You can wash your feet, spend the night, and be on your way in the morning.” They told him, “No, we'll spend the night in the city square.”
text: Me wuranom, mesrɛ mo, mommane mmra mo akoa fie mmɛsoɛ anadwo yi, na monhohoro mo nan ase, na anɔpatutu moasɔre akɔ mo kwan. Na wɔse: Dabi, yɛbɛda abɔntene so.
translated: I am telling you, I am warning you, let your heart be strong and courageous today, and do not be afraid, and do not tremble, and the Lord your God will go before you. And they said: Dabi, we will cross the river.

ref: Jacob heard that Laban's sons were complaining, “Jacob is now a rich man, and he got everything he owns from our father.”
text: Na ɔtee Laban mmammarima nsɛm a wɔkaeɛ no sɛ: Yakob afa deɛ ɛyɛ yɛn agya deɛ nyinaa; na ɛno na ɔde anya saa agyapadeɛ yi nyinaa.
translated: Now Laban had two daughters; the name of the elder was Leah, and the name of the younger was Rachel

ref: Hirah asked the people of Enaim, “Where is the prostitute who sat along the road outside your town?” “There's never been one here,” they answered.

ref: “My son,” Isaac answered, “I have made Jacob the ruler over you and your brothers, and all of you will be his servants. I have also promised him all the grain and grapes that he needs. There's nothing left that I can do for you.”
text: Na Isak bua see Esau sɛ: Hwɛ, mede no mayɛ wo so wura, na mede ne nuanom nyinaa mayɛ ne nkoa. Na mede aburoo ne nsa mawowa no. Na afei wo deɛ, ɛdeɛn na menyɛ woɔ, me ba?
translated: And Isaac said to Esau: Behold, I have made him thy lord, and all his brethren have I given to him for servants; and with corn and wine have I sustained him: and what shall I do now unto thee, my son?

translated: And Isaac said to his father, "Let me smell the smell of your clothes, and then I will bless you, for the smell of the clothes is like the smell of the field that the Lord has blessed."
